citations.note: Redistribution and use in source and binary forms, with or without modification, are permitted
Redistribution and use ... with or without modification, are permitted
Redistribution and use in source and binary forms ... are permitted provided that the following conditions are met
Broad permission for any use; no restriction on private use.
Redistributions of source code must retain the above copyright notice, this list of conditions and the following disclaimer
Redistributions in binary form must reproduce the above copyright notice, this list of conditions and the following disclaimer in the documentation
Source redistributions must retain the above copyright notice
Binary redistributions must reproduce the above copyright notice in the documentation

The 'advertising clause': All advertising materials mentioning features or use of this software must display the following acknowledgement: This product includes software developed by the organization.

external_references.summary: FSF notes that the 'obnoxious BSD advertising clause' creates a practical problem: linking many independently BSD-4-licensed components multiplies the number of required acknowledgements, producing impractically long notices. This is why the FSF and the Berkeley team itself eventually moved away from the 4-clause form.

conditions.commentary: The signature and controversial feature of BSD 4-Clause: clause 3 (s-8) strengthens attribution beyond what a copyright notice normally requires. Every piece of advertising material that mentions features or use of the software must carry an acknowledgement of the organization that developed it. When many 4-clause-licensed components are combined, the required acknowledgements accumulate, which is why this form was abandoned in favor of the 3-clause 'modified' BSD and why the FSF classifies it as non-free.

citations.note: IN NO EVENT SHALL COPYRIGHT HOLDER BE LIABLE FOR ANY DIRECT, INDIRECT, INCIDENTAL, SPECIAL, EXEMPLARY, OR CONSEQUENTIAL DAMAGES
THIS SOFTWARE IS PROVIDED BY COPYRIGHT HOLDER "AS IS" AND ANY EXPRESS OR IMPLIED WARRANTIES ... ARE DISCLAIMED
Neither the name of the copyright holder nor the names of its contributors may be used to endorse or promote products derived from this software without specific prior written permission

limitations.commentary: Clause 4 (the 'no-endorsement' clause) bars use of the names of the copyright holder or contributors to endorse or promote derivative products without written permission. This is the narrow trademark/name-use restriction also present in BSD 3-Clause.
